title: I Would In That Sweet Bosom Be
content:
I would in that sweet bosom be
    (O sweet it is and fair it is!)
    Where no rude wind might visit me.
    Because of sad austerities
    I

title: Rain Has Fallen All The Day
content:
Rain has fallen all the day.
    O come among the laden trees:
    The leaves lie thick upon the way
    Of memories.

    Staying a little by

title: Watching The Needleboats At San Sabba
content:
I heard their young hearts crying
    Loveward above the glancing oar
    And heard the prairie grasses sighing:
    No more, return no more!

title: When The Shy Star Goes Forth In Heaven
content:
When the shy star goes forth in heaven
    All maidenly, disconsolate,
    Hear you amid the drowsy even
    One who is singing by your gate.

title: Tilly
content:
He travels after a winter sun,
    Urging the cattle along a cold red road,
    Calling to them, a voice they know,
    He drives his beasts ab

title: Go Seek Her Out All Courteously
content:
Go seek her out all courteously,
    And say I come,
    Wind of spices whose song is ever
    Epithalamium.
    O, hurry over the dark lands

title: Dear Heart, Why Will You Use Me So?
content:
Dear heart, why will you use me so?
    Dear eyes that gently me upbraid,
    Still are you beautiful, but O,
    How is your beauty raimented!

title: On The Beach At Fontana
content:
Wind whines and whines the shingle,
    The crazy pierstakes groan;
    A senile sea numbers each single
    Slimesilvered stone.

    From wh

title: Lean Out Of The Window
content:
Lean out of the window,
    Goldenhair,
    I hear you singing
    A merry air.

    My book was closed,
    I read no more,
    Watching th

title: The Twilight Turns From Amethyst
content:
The twilight turns from amethyst
    To deep and deeper blue,
    The lamp fills with a pale green glow
    The trees of the avenue.

    The

title: Bahnhofstrasse
content:
The eyes that mock me sign the way
    Whereto I pass at eve of day.

    Grey way whose violet signals are
    The trysting and the twining st

title: Gentle Lady, Do Not Sing
content:
Gentle lady, do not sing
    Sad songs about the end of love;
    Lay aside sadness and sing
    How love that passes is enough.

    Sing abo

title: My Dove, My Beautiful One
content:
My dove, my beautiful one,
    Arise, arise!
    The night-dew lies
    Upon my lips and eyes.

    The odorous winds are weaving
    A music

title: Strings In The Earth And Air
content:
Strings in the earth and air
    Make music sweet;
    Strings by the river where
    The willows meet.

    There�s music along the river

title: Sleep Now, O Sleep Now
content:
Sleep now, O sleep now,
    O you unquiet heart!

    Is heard in my heart.

title: Flood
content:
Goldbrown upon the sated flood
    The rockvine clusters lift and sway;
    Vast wings above the lambent waters brood
    Of sullen day.

title: A Memory Of The Players In A Mirror At Midnight
content:
They mouth love's language. Gnash
    The thirteen teeth
    Your lean jaws grin with. Lash
    Your itch and quailing, nude greed of the flesh

title: O, It Was Out By Donnycarney
content:
O, it was out by Donnycarney
    When the bat flew from tree to tree
    My love and I did walk together;
    And sweet were the words she said

title: Lightly Come Or Lightly Go
content:
Lightly come or lightly go:
    Though thy heart presage thee woe,
    Vales and many a wasted sun,
    Oread let thy laughter run,

title: Ecce Puer
content:
Of the dark past
    A child is born;
    With joy and grief
    My heart is torn.

    Calm in his cradle
    The living lies.
    May love

title: Bid Adieu, Adieu, Adieu
content:
Bid adieu, adieu, adieu,
    Bid adieu to girlish days,
    Happy Love is come to woo
    Thee and woo thy girlish ways,

title: The Ballad Of Persse O'Reilly
content:

    And curled up like Lord Olofa Crumple

title: In The Dark Pine-Wood
content:
In the dark pine-wood
    I would we lay,
    In deep cool shadow
    At noon of day.

    How sweet to lie there,
    Sweet to kiss,
    Wh

title: Alone
content:
The noon's greygolden meshes make
    All night a veil,
    The shorelamps in the sleeping lake
    Laburnum tendrils trail.

    The sly reed

title: O Cool Is The Valley Now
content:
O cool is the valley now
    And there, love, will we go
    For many a choir is singing now
    Where Love did sometime go.
    And hear you

title: O Sweetheart, Hear You
content:
O Sweetheart, hear you
    Your lover�s tale;
    A man shall have sorrow
    When friends him fail.

    For he shall know then
    Friends

title: I Hear An Army Charging Upon The Land
content:
I hear an army charging upon the land,
    And the thunder of horses plunging, foam about their knees:
    Arrogant, in black armour, behind the

title: Be Not Sad Because All Men
content:
Be not sad because all men
    Prefer a lying clamour before you:
    Sweetheart, be at peace again,
    Can they dishonour you?

    They are

title: Of That So Sweet Imprisonment
content:
Of that so sweet imprisonment
    My soul, dearest, is fain,
    Soft arms that woo me to relent
    And woo me to detain.
    Ah, could they

title: Nightpiece
content:
Gaunt in gloom,
    The pale stars their torches,
    Enshrouded, wave.
    Ghostfires from heaven's far verges faint illume,
    Arches on so

title: Though I Thy Mithridates Were
content:
Though I thy Mithridates were,
    Framed to defy the poison-dart,
    Yet must thou fold me unaware
    To know the rapture of thy heart,

title: Simples
content:
O bella bionda,
    Sei come l'onda!


    Of cool sweet dew and radiance mild
    The moon a web of silence weaves

title: What Counsel Has The Hooded Moon
content:
What counsel has the hooded moon
    Put in thy heart, my shyly sweet,
    Of Love in ancient plenilune,
    Glory and stars beneath his feet,

title: A Flower Given To My Daughter
content:
Frail the white rose and frail are
    Her hands that gave
    Whose soul is sere and paler
    Than time's wan wave.

    Rosefrail and fair,

title: Who Goes Amid The Green Wood
content:
Who goes amid the green wood
    With springtide all adorning her?
    Who goes amid the merry green wood
    To make it merrier?

    Who pas

title: She Weeps Over Rahoon
content:
Rain on Rahoon falls softly, softly falling,
    Where my dark lover lies.
    Sad is his voice that calls me, sadly calling,
    At grey moonr

title: Now, O Now, In This Brown Land
content:
Now, O now, in this brown land
    Where Love did so sweet music make
    We two shall wander, hand in hand,
    Forbearing for old friendship�

title: Tutto � Sciolto
content:
A birdless heaven, seadusk, one lone star
    Piercing the west,
    As thou, fond heart, love's time, so faint, so far,
    Rememberest.

title: Love Came To Us In Time Gone By
content:
Love came to us in time gone by
    When one at twilight shyly played
    And one in fear was standing nigh,
    For Love at first is all afrai

title: All Day I Hear The Noise Of Waters
content:
All day I hear the noise of waters
    Making moan,
    Sad as the sea-bird is when, going
    Forth alone,
    He hears the winds cry to the

title: A Prayer
content:
Again!
    Come, give, yield all your strength to me!
    From far a low word breathes on the breaking brain

title: Silently She�s Combing
content:
Silently she�s combing,
    Combing her long hair
    Silently and graciously,
    With many a pretty air.

    The sun is in the willow leave

title: Bright Cap And Streamers
content:
Bright cap and streamers,
    He sings in the hollow:
    Come follow, come follow,
    All you that love.
    Leave dreams to the dreamers

title: This Heart That Flutters Near My Heart
content:
This heart that flutters near my heart
    My hope and all my riches is,
    Unhappy when we draw apart
    And happy between kiss and kiss:

title: From Dewy Dreams, My Soul, Arise
content:
From dewy dreams, my soul, arise,

    For lo! the treees are full of sighs

title: Because Your Voice Was At My Side
content:
Because your voice was at my side
    I gave him pain,
    Because within my hand I held
    Your hand again.

    There is no word nor any si

title: My Love Is In A Light Attire
content:
My love is in a light attire
    Among the apple-trees,
    Where the gay winds do most desire
    To run in companies.

    There, where the

title: At That Hour When All Things Have Repose
content:
At that hour when all things have repose,
    O lonely watcher of the skies,
    Do you hear the night wind and the sighs
    Of harps playing

title: Thou Leanest To The Shell Of Night
content:
Thou leanest to the shell of night,
    Dear lady, a divining ear.
    In that soft choiring of delight
    What sound hath made thy heart to f

title: Winds Of May
content:
Winds of May, that dance on the sea,
    Dancing a ring-around in glee
    From furrow to furrow, while overhead
    The foam flies up to be ga

title: He Who Hath Glory Lost, Nor Hath
content:
He who hath glory lost, nor hath
    Found any soul to fellow his,
    Among his foes in scorn and wrath
    Holding to ancient nobleness,
